verses.latin_vulgate: Onus vallis Visionis.   Quidnam quoque tibi est, quia ascendisti  et tu omnis in tecta ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשָּׂ֖א גֵּ֣יא חִזָּי֑וֹן מַה־לָּ֣ךְ אֵפ֔וֹא כִּי־עָלִ֥ית כֻּלָּ֖ךְ לַגַּגּֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The burden of the valley of vision. What aileth thee also, that thou too art wholly gone up to the housetops?
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on Näkylaakson kuorma: mikä sinun nyt on, että te niin kaikki olette astuneet kattoin päälle?
verses.king_james: The burden of the valley of vision. What aileth thee now, that thou art wholly gone up to the housetops?

verses.latin_vulgate: Clamoris plena, urbs frequens,  civitas exsultans ;  interfecti tui, non interfecti gladio,  nec mortui in bello.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּשֻׁא֣וֹת ׀ מְלֵאָ֗ה עִ֚יר הֽוֹמִיָּ֔ה קִרְיָ֖ה עַלִּיזָ֑ה חֲלָלַ֙יִךְ֙ לֹ֣א חַלְלֵי־חֶ֔רֶב וְלֹ֖א מֵתֵ֥י מִלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Full of clamour, a populous city, a joyous city: thy slain are not slain by the sword, nor dead in battle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet täynnä humua: kaupunki täynnä väkeä, iloinen kaupunki: sinun lyötys ei ole miekalla lyödyt, ja ei ole kuolleet sodassa.
verses.king_james: Thou that art full of stirs, a tumultuous city, a joyous city: thy slain men are not slain with the sword, nor dead in battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cuncti principes tui fugerunt simul  dureque ligati sunt ;  omnes qui inventi sunt vincti sunt pariter ;  procul fugerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־קְצִינַ֥יִךְ נָֽדְדוּ־יַ֖חַד מִקֶּ֣שֶׁת אֻסָּ֑רוּ כׇּל־נִמְצָאַ֙יִךְ֙ אֻסְּר֣וּ יַחְדָּ֔ו מֵרָח֖וֹק בָּרָֽחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: All the princes are fled together, and are bound hard: all that were found, are bound together, they are fled far off.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan kaikki sinun päämiehes ovat poikenneet joutsesta pois, ja ovat vangitut; kaikki kuin sinussa löyttiin, ovat vangitut, ja kauvas paenneet.
verses.king_james: All thy rulers are fled together, they are bound by the archers: all that are found in thee are bound together, which have fled from far.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea dixi : Recedite a me :  amare flebo ;  nolite incumbere ut consolemini me  super vastitate filiæ populi mei ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֥ן אָמַ֛רְתִּי שְׁע֥וּ מִנִּ֖י אֲמָרֵ֣ר בַּבֶּ֑כִי אַל־תָּאִ֣יצוּ לְנַֽחֲמֵ֔נִי עַל־שֹׁ֖ד בַּת־עַמִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore have I said: Depart from me, I will weep bitterly: labour not to comfort me, for the devastation of the daughter of my people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden minä sanon: menkäät pois minun tyköäni, antakaat minun itkeä vaikiasti; älkäät ahkeroitko lohduttaa, minun kansani tyttären hävityksen tähden.
verses.king_james: Therefore said I, Look away from me; I will weep bitterly, labour not to comfort me, because of the spoiling of the daughter of my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: dies enim interfectionis,  et conculcationis, et fletuum,  Domino Deo exercituum,  in valle Visionis,  scrutans murum,  et magnificus super montem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י יוֹם֩ מְהוּמָ֨ה וּמְבוּסָ֜ה וּמְבוּכָ֗ה לַֽאדֹנָ֧י יֱהֹוִ֛ה צְבָא֖וֹת בְּגֵ֣י חִזָּי֑וֹן מְקַרְקַ֥ר קִ֖ר וְשׁ֥וֹעַ אֶל־הָהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: For it is a day of slaughter and of treading down, and of weeping to the Lord the God of hosts in the valley of vision, searching the wall, and magnificent upon the mountain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä se on kapinan, sortamisen ja surun päivä Herralta, Herralta Zebaotilta, Näkylaaksossa muurien kukistamisen tähden, ja huudon tähden vuorella;
verses.king_james: For it is a day of trouble, and of treading down, and of perplexity by the Lord God of hosts in the valley of vision, breaking down the walls, and of crying to the mountains.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Ælam sumpsit pharetram,  currum hominis equitis,  et parietem nudavit clypeus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעֵילָם֙ נָשָׂ֣א אַשְׁפָּ֔ה בְּרֶ֥כֶב אָדָ֖ם פָּרָשִׁ֑ים וְקִ֥יר עֵרָ֖ה מָגֵֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elam took the quiver, the chariot of the horseman, and the shield was taken down from the wall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että Elam lähtee viinellä, vaunuilla, väellä, ratsasmiehillä, ja Kir välkkyy kilvistä.
verses.king_james: And Elam bare the quiver with chariots of men and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erunt electæ valles tuæ plenæ quadrigarum,  et equites ponent sedes suas in porta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֥י מִבְחַר־עֲמָקַ֖יִךְ מָ֣לְאוּ רָ֑כֶב וְהַפָּ֣רָשִׁ֔ים שֹׁ֖ת שָׁ֥תוּ הַשָּֽׁעְרָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thy choice valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall place themselves in the gate.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtuu, että sinun valitut laaksos ovat täynnä vaunuja; ja ratsastajat sioittavat itsensä portin eteen.
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass, that thy choicest valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall set themselves in array at the gate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et revelabitur operimentum Judæ,  et videbis in die illa  armamentarium domus saltus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְגַ֕ל אֵ֖ת מָסַ֣ךְ יְהוּדָ֑ה וַתַּבֵּט֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא אֶל־נֶ֖שֶׁק בֵּ֥ית הַיָּֽעַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the covering of Juda shall be discovered, and thou shalt see in that day the armoury of the house of the forest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin pitää Juudan peite avattaman, että aseet nähdään silloin metsähuoneessa.
verses.king_james: And he discovered the covering of Judah, and thou didst look in that day to the armour of the house of the forest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et scissuras civitatis David videbitis,  quia multiplicatæ sunt ;  et congregastis aquas piscinæ inferioris,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֵ֨ת בְּקִיעֵ֧י עִיר־דָּוִ֛ד רְאִיתֶ֖ם כִּי־רָ֑בּוּ וַֽתְּקַבְּצ֔וּ אֶת־מֵ֥י הַבְּרֵכָ֖ה הַתַּחְתּוֹנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you shall see the breaches of the city of David, that they are many: and you have gathered together the waters of the lower pool,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja te olette näkevä monta reikää Davidin kaupungissa; ja kokootte veden alimmaiseen lammikkoon.
verses.king_james: Ye have seen also the breaches of the city of David, that they are many: and ye gathered together the waters of the lower pool.

verses.latin_vulgate: et domos Jerusalem numerastis,  et destruxistis domos ad muniendum murum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־בָּתֵּ֥י יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם סְפַרְתֶּ֑ם וַתִּתְצוּ֙ הַבָּ֣תִּ֔ים לְבַצֵּ֖ר הַחוֹמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and broken down houses to fortify the wall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Teidän pitää lukeman huoneet Jerusalemissa, ja teidän pitää jaottaman huoneet vahvistaaksenne muureja.
verses.king_james: And ye have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and the houses have ye broken down to fortify the wall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et lacum fecistis inter duos muros  ad aquam piscinæ veteris ;  et non suspexistis ad eum qui fecerat eam,  et operatorem ejus de longe non vidistis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִקְוָ֣ה ׀ עֲשִׂיתֶ֗ם בֵּ֚ין הַחֹ֣מֹתַ֔יִם לְמֵ֖י הַבְּרֵכָ֣ה הַיְשָׁנָ֑ה וְלֹ֤א הִבַּטְתֶּם֙ אֶל־עֹשֶׂ֔יהָ וְיֹצְרָ֥הּ מֵרָח֖וֹק לֹ֥א רְאִיתֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you made a ditch between the two walls for the water of the old pool: and you have not looked up to the maker thereof, nor regarded him even at a distance, that wrought it long ago.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja teidän pitää tekemän vesihaudan molempain muurien välille, vanhan kalalammin vedestä; kuitenkin ette katso häntä, joka tämän tekee, ettekä katsele sitä, joka senkaltaisia on kaukaa toimittanut.
verses.king_james: Ye made also a ditch between the two walls for the water of the old pool: but ye have not looked unto the maker thereof, neither had respect unto him that fashioned it long ago.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vocabit Dominus Deus exercituum  in die illa  ad fletum, et ad planctum,  ad calvitium, et ad cingulum sacci ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֗א אֲדֹנָ֧י יֱהֹוִ֛ה צְבָא֖וֹת בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא לִבְכִי֙ וּלְמִסְפֵּ֔ד וּלְקׇרְחָ֖ה וְלַחֲגֹ֥ר שָֽׂק׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord, the God of hosts, in that day shall call to weeping, and to mourning, to baldness, and to girding with sackcloth:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden antaa Herra, Herra Zebaot, silloin julistaa, että itketään ja murehditaan, ja hiukset ajellaan ja puetaan säkkiin.
verses.king_james: And in that day did the Lord God of hosts call to weeping, and to mourning, and to baldness, and to girding with sackcloth:

verses.latin_vulgate: et ecce gaudium et lætitia,  occidere vitulos et jugulare arietes,  comedere carnes, et bibere vinum :  comedamus et bibamus,  cras enim moriemur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִנֵּ֣ה ׀ שָׂשׂ֣וֹן וְשִׂמְחָ֗ה הָרֹ֤ג ׀ בָּקָר֙ וְשָׁחֹ֣ט צֹ֔אן אָכֹ֥ל בָּשָׂ֖ר וְשָׁת֣וֹת יָ֑יִן אָכ֣וֹל וְשָׁת֔וֹ כִּ֥י מָחָ֖ר נָמֽוּת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And behold joy and gladness, killing calves, and slaying rams, eating flesh, and drinking wine: Let us eat and drink; for to morrow we shall die.
verses.finnish_biblia: (Vaikka nyt) katso, ilo ja riemu, teurastaa härkiä, tappaa lampaita, syödä lihaa, juoda viinaa, (ja sanoa): syökäämme ja juokaamme, meidän pitää kuitenkin huomenna kuoleman.
verses.king_james: And behold joy and gladness, slaying oxen, and killing sheep, eating flesh, and drinking wine: let us eat and drink; for to morrow we shall die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et revelata est in auribus meis vox Domini exercituum :  Si dimittetur iniquitas hæc vobis donec moriamini,  dicit Dominus Deus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִגְלָ֥ה בְאׇזְנָ֖י יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֑וֹת אִם־יְ֠כֻפַּ֠ר הֶעָוֺ֨ן הַזֶּ֤ה לָכֶם֙ עַד־תְּמֻת֔וּן אָמַ֛ר אֲדֹנָ֥י יֱהֹוִ֖ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the voice of the Lord of hosts was revealed in my ears: Surely this iniquity shall not be forgiven you till you die, saith the Lord God of hosts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on, (sanoo) Herra Zebaot, minun korvilleni tullut: mitämaks, jos minä tämän pahan teon annan anteeksi, siihenasti kuin te kuolette, sanoo Herra, Herra Zebaot.
verses.king_james: And it was revealed in mine ears by the Lord of hosts, Surely this iniquity shall not be purged from you till ye die, saith the Lord God of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus Deus exercituum :  Vade, ingredere ad eum qui habitat in tabernaculo,  ad Sobnam, præpositum templi, et dices ad eum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֥ה אָמַ֛ר אֲדֹנָ֥י יֱהֹוִ֖ה צְבָא֑וֹת לֶךְ־בֹּא֙ אֶל־הַסֹּכֵ֣ן הַזֶּ֔ה עַל־שֶׁבְנָ֖א אֲשֶׁ֥ר עַל־הַבָּֽיִת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thus saith the Lord God of hosts: Go, get thee in to him that dwelleth in the tabernacle, to Sobna who is over the temple: and thou shalt say to him:
verses.finnish_biblia: Näin sanoo Herra, Herra Zebaot: mene tavarahuoneen haltian Sebnan tykö ja sano hänelle:
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord God of hosts, Go, get thee unto this treasurer, even unto Shebna, which is over the house, and say,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid tu hic, aut quasi quis hic ?  quia excidisti tibi hic sepulchrum,  excidisti in excelso memoriale diligenter,  in petra tabernaculum tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־לְּךָ֥ פֹה֙ וּמִ֣י לְךָ֣ פֹ֔ה כִּֽי־חָצַ֧בְתָּ לְּךָ֛ פֹּ֖ה קָ֑בֶר חֹצְבִ֤י מָרוֹם֙ קִבְר֔וֹ חֹקְקִ֥י בַסֶּ֖לַע מִשְׁכָּ֥ן לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: What dost thou here, or as if thou wert somebody here? for thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, thou hast hewed out a monument carefully in a high place, a dwelling for thyself in a rock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä sinun tässä on, kenenkä oma sinä olet? ettäs annat hakata itselles haudan niinkuin se, joka hakkauttaa itsellensä haudan korkeuteen, ja olet niinkuin se, joka antaa itsellensä tehdä asumasian vuorelle?
verses.king_james: What hast thou here? and whom hast thou here, that thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, as he that heweth him out a sepulchre on high, and that graveth an habitation for himself in a rock?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce Dominus asportari te faciet,  sicut asportatur gallus gallinaceus ;  et quasi amictum, sic sublevabit te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ מְטַלְטֶלְךָ֔ טַלְטֵלָ֖ה גָּ֑בֶר וְעֹטְךָ֖ עָטֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold the Lord will cause thee to be carried away, as a cock is carried away, and he will lift thee up as a garment.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, Herra ajaa sinun pois, niinkuin väkevä mies heittää jonkun pois, ja peittää sinun kokonansa,
verses.king_james: Behold, the Lord will carry thee away with a mighty captivity, and will surely cover thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Coronas coronabit te tribulatione ;  quasi pilam mittet te in terram latam et spatiosam ;  ibi morieris, et ibi erit currus gloriæ tuæ,  ignominia domus domini tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צָנ֤וֹף יִצְנׇפְךָ֙ צְנֵפָ֔ה כַּדּ֕וּר אֶל־אֶ֖רֶץ רַחֲבַ֣ת יָדָ֑יִם שָׁ֣מָּה תָמ֗וּת וְשָׁ֙מָּה֙ מַרְכְּב֣וֹת כְּבוֹדֶ֔ךָ קְל֖וֹן בֵּ֥ית אֲדֹנֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He will crown thee with a crown of tribulation, he will toss thee like a ball into a large and spacious country: there shalt thou die, and there shall the chariot of thy glory be, the shame of the house of thy Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja pyörittää sinun kohta, ja peräti niinkuin kierikan aukialle kedolle; siellä sinun pitää kuoleman, siellä pitää sinun kalliit vaunus, sinun Herras huoneen kanssa, tuleman häväistykseksi.
verses.king_james: He will surely violently turn and toss thee like a ball into a large country: there shalt thou die, and there the chariots of thy glory shall be the shame of thy lord’s house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et expellam te de statione tua,  et de ministerio tuo deponam te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַהֲדַפְתִּ֖יךָ מִמַּצָּבֶ֑ךָ וּמִמַּעֲמָֽדְךָ֖ יֶהֶרְסֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will drive thee out from thy station, and depose thee from thy ministry.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä kukistan sinun säädystäs, ja sinä pannaan pois viraltas.
verses.king_james: And I will drive thee from thy station, and from thy state shall he pull thee down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit in die illa :  vocabo servum meum Eliacim, filium Helciæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֖ה בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא וְקָרָ֣אתִי לְעַבְדִּ֔י לְאֶלְיָקִ֖ים בֶּן־חִלְקִיָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will call my servant Eliacim the son of Helcias,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja silloin pitää tapahtuman, että minä kutsun palveliani Eliakimin, Hilkian pojan,
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will call my servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah:

verses.latin_vulgate: et induam illum tunica tua,  et cingulo tuo confortabo eum,  et potestatem tuam dabo in manu ejus ;  et erit quasi pater habitantibus Jerusalem  et domui Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִלְבַּשְׁתִּ֣יו כֻּתׇּנְתֶּ֗ךָ וְאַבְנֵֽטְךָ֙ אֲחַזְּקֶ֔נּוּ וּמֶֽמְשַׁלְתְּךָ֖ אֶתֵּ֣ן בְּיָד֑וֹ וְהָיָ֥ה לְאָ֛ב לְיוֹשֵׁ֥ב יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם וּלְבֵ֥ית יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will clothe him with thy robe, and will strengthen him with thy girdle, and will give thy power into his hand: and he shall be as a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja panen hänen yllensä sinun hamees, ja vyötän hänen sinun vyölläs, ja annan sinun valtas hänelle; että hän on niiden isä, jotka Jerusalemissa ja Juudan huoneessa asuvat.
verses.king_james: And I will clothe him with thy robe, and strengthen him with thy girdle, and I will commit thy government into his hand: and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dabo clavem domus David  super humerum ejus ;  et aperiet, et non erit qui claudat ;  et claudet, et non erit qui aperiat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָתַתִּ֛י מַפְתֵּ֥חַ בֵּית־דָּוִ֖ד עַל־שִׁכְמ֑וֹ וּפָתַח֙ וְאֵ֣ין סֹגֵ֔ר וְסָגַ֖ר וְאֵ֥ין פֹּתֵֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will lay the key of the house of David upon his shoulder: and he shall open, and none shall shut: and he shall shut, and none shall open.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja panen Davidin huoneen avaimen hänen olallensa, että hän avaa, ja ei kenkään sulje, että hän sulkee, ja ei kenkään avaa.
verses.king_james: And the key of the house of David will I lay upon his shoulder; so he shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et figam illum paxillum in loco fideli,  et erit in solium gloriæ domui patris ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּתְקַעְתִּ֥יו יָתֵ֖ד בְּמָק֣וֹם נֶאֱמָ֑ן וְהָיָ֛ה לְכִסֵּ֥א כָב֖וֹד לְבֵ֥ית אָבִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will fasten him as a peg in a sure place, and he shall be for a throne of glory to the house of his father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lyön hänen niinkuin naulan vahvaan paikkaan; ja hänellä on oleva kunnian istuin, hänen isänsä huoneessa,
verses.king_james: And I will fasten him as a nail in a sure place; and he shall be for a glorious throne to his father’s house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et suspendent super eum omnem gloriam domus patris ejus ;  vasorum diversa genera,  omne vas parvulum,  a vasis craterarum usque ad omne vas musicorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְתָל֨וּ עָלָ֜יו כֹּ֣ל ׀ כְּב֣וֹד בֵּית־אָבִ֗יו הַצֶּֽאֱצָאִים֙ וְהַצְּפִע֔וֹת כֹּ֖ל כְּלֵ֣י הַקָּטָ֑ן מִכְּלֵי֙ הָאַגָּנ֔וֹת וְעַ֖ד כׇּל־כְּלֵ֥י הַנְּבָלִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father's house, divers kinds of vessels, every little vessel, from the vessels of cups even to every instrument of music.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että hänen päällensä ripustetaan kaikki hänen isänsä huoneen kunnia, lapset ja lasten lapset, kaikki pienet kappaleet, juoma-astiat, ja kaikkinaiset kanteleet.
verses.king_james: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father’s house, the offspring and the issue, all vessels of small quantity, from the vessels of cups, even to all the vessels of flagons.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa, dicit Dominus exercituum,  auferetur paxillus qui fixus fuerat in loco fideli,  et frangetur, et cadet,  et peribit quod pependerat in eo,  quia Dominus locutus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֗וּא נְאֻם֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת תָּמוּשׁ֙ הַיָּתֵ֔ד הַתְּקוּעָ֖ה בְּמָק֣וֹם נֶאֱמָ֑ן וְנִגְדְּעָ֣ה וְנָפְלָ֗ה וְנִכְרַת֙ הַמַּשָּׂ֣א אֲשֶׁר־עָלֶ֔יהָ כִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּֽר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: In that day, saith the Lord of hosts, shall the peg be removed, that was fastened in the sure place: and it shall be broken and shall fall: and that which hung thereon, shall perish, because the Lord hath spoken it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin, sanoo Herra Zebaot, naula pitää otettaman pois, joka vahvassa paikassa on, niin että hänen pitää rikkoontuman, lankeeman, putooman, ja se kuorma kuin hänen päällänsä on, pitää hukkuman; sillä Herra sen sanoo.
verses.king_james: In that day, saith the Lord of hosts, shall the nail that is fastened in the sure place be removed, and be cut down, and fall; and the burden that was upon it shall be cut off: for the Lord hath spoken it.
